design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate multi-floored levels, defeat enemy waves or solve puzzles to unlock new areas, face boss encounters at stage ends. Health regenerates via healing factor between encounters.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure (Elsie-Dee timer forcing player forward) vs. exploration/puzzle-solving for area progression. Healing factor creates risk/reward of lingering vs. pushing forward.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Wolverine—exploring his mysterious past through combat-focused progression, utilizing his signature claw attacks and healing abilities while confronting iconic X-Men villains.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Early adoption of recharging health system (healing factor) instead of traditional item pickups. SNES version emphasizes mandatory enemy destruction as progression gate; Genesis version prioritizes combat flexibility and puzzle navigation. Time limit with named NPC consequence (Elsie-Dee) creates narrative tension around pacing.

difficulty.lives_system: Genesis: 5 lives per session. SNES: Game Over on time limit expiration (no explicit lives mentioned).
difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows continue at any unlocked level.
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint system exists (Genesis version has countdown timer reset at checkpoints). SNES has slower health recovery; Genesis includes visual timer indicating Elsie-Dee proximity.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Password system grants players full control over which level to attempt. Health regeneration reduces punishment for minor damage, but time limit creates forced pacing pressure. SNES requires enemy destruction count before progression; Genesis offers more flexible puzzle-based navigation.

progression.upgrade_system: Wolverine's move sets differ between versions but no traditional upgrade tree documented. Moves are version-locked rather than earned through progression.
progression.unlock_mechanism: SNES: Enemy destruction count gates unlock to next area. Genesis: Puzzle-solving and level navigation gates progression. Both versions: Boss defeat advances story.
replayability_hooks: Password system enables level select and speedrun attempts
Different move sets between Genesis and SNES versions encourage replaying on alternate platform
Boss encounters differ between versions, providing unique challenges per platform

world_building_techniques: Linear stage progression following mystery of Wolverine's past (Weapon X experimentation backstory)
Character encounters as level progression: meeting Storm, defeating Lady Deathstrike, Sabretooth, Shinobi Shaw across stages
Villain reveals (Cyber's hallucinogenic nightmare trap, Inner Circle conspiracy, final mastermind Great Beast/The Hand)
Narrative closure: return to X-Mansion with X-Men greeting after defeating final antagonist

boss_design.philosophy: Each stage culminates in thematic boss confrontation tied to story progression. Bosses represent plot antagonists (Lady Deathstrike, Sabretooth, Cyber, Selene, Fugue, Shaw, Wendigo, Fitzroy, final Beast). Boss rosters differ between Genesis and SNES versions.
notable_bosses: Lady Deathstrike (appears both versions, delivers plot revelation)
Cyber (pharmacological trap: hallucinogenic nightmare requiring player to reverse toxin)
Shinobi Shaw (central antagonist, defeated mid-game but continues into catacombs)
Great Beast/The Hand leader (final boss, true mastermind)

faction_indicators: Weapon X program (institutional oppression against Wolverine)
The Inner Circle (hidden power structure using Shaw as front)
The Hand (true puppet masters, mystical organization)
X-Men (allies offering help; Wolverine refuses, choosing solitary path—character stubbornness as plot driver)
danger_signals: Mysterious anonymous contact luring Wolverine into trap
Shinobi Shaw's contract on life (assassin economy active)
Cyber's poison turning Wolverine's own biology against him (vulnerability despite healing factor)
Multiple pursuers across territories (Canada → Japan → NYC) indicating organized conspiracy
environmental_storytelling: Weapon X facility level establishes horrific past via architecture/presence
Mountain and jungle traversal emphasizes isolation (refusing X-Men aid)
Inner Circle mansion descent into catacombs (literal uncovering of hidden truth)
Return to X-Mansion epilogue (cyclical narrative, found family resolution)
